|. TACOMA, May 16.--Tyee Dick, or
Richard Tinawali, the descenda mt
and the last male representative OF a
long line of Puyallup kings, IL dead.
at the age OF 80 years. at his home
on the reservation, Tinawali has
been very feeble for some time. and
to his decrepitude were added many
sorrows at the death Of large fam
lily OF children, with the exception
of two girls, who survive him.


Tinawali In his day was a power
IN the councils of tho Puyallups. He
was an eloquent orator In his youth
and manhood. He was also a daring
hunter and skillful at Indian games
He might have been great warrior,
but he possessed o mild and mag
unanimous spirit. and his eloquent
tongue was generally raised for
peace.


Tinawali 10 one OF the last OF the
long list of red men who signed the
Medicine creek treaty In the SOS.
HUT ono other Puyaliup Indian. John
Harton. now living near Tenino, In
alive. who signed that treaty, and
he 10 80 old ana feeble that his death
10 expected at my tlme
